sections.paragraph:
ἀκρίβεια, ας, ἡ strict conformity to a norm or standard, exactness, precision (so Thu. et al. [s. Renehan ’75, 19]; pap since III b.c.: e.g. UPZ 110, 46 [164 b.c.]; POxy 471, 11ff [I a.d.]; 237 VIII, 39 [II a.d.]; also s. Preis.; LXX; En 106:12; EpArist 103; Philo; Jos., Ant. 4, 309; 9, 208 al.; Tat.; Mel., HE 4, 26, 13) πεπαιδευμένος κατὰ ἀ. τοῦ πατρῴου νόμου educated strictly according to our ancestral law Ac 22:3 (Isocr. 7, 40 ἀ. νόμων; Jos., Vi. 191). ABaumgarten, The Name of the Pharisees: JBL 102, ’83, 411–28.—DELG s.v. ἀκριβής. M-M. Sv.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Renehan Renehan = RR., Greek Lexicographical Notes—List 6

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 39
